refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to operators and system managers.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.
DP2: Use redundant communication channels to ensure packet delivery.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and feedback control loop.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive bitrate and error correction algorithms to reduce packet loss.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses the FR with minimal coupling to other system functions; adaptive bitrate and error correction are modular and can be encapsulated.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that adaptive algorithms may introduce latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating latency monitoring as a complementary FR to mitigate risk.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and communication stack responsible for teleoperation control and communication quality management.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and status to the NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but teleoperation communication quality logic is diffused if placed here.
Vehicle module is primarily physical control; low containment relevance for communication quality.
High containment score as this module owns teleoperation communication; low diffusion risk.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety policies, less relevant for communication quality.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication quality management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Coordinate with Safety Services to ensure communication quality issues trigger appropriate safety alerts.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear verification methods and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support assignment to Teleoperation module, ensuring clear ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data and network link monitoring within the Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add complementary FR for latency monitoring to address risk of adaptive bitrate latency impact.
Establish interface protocols with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition is sufficient with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment meeting containment and verification criteria. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report communication quality status to operators and system managers.

revise.why: Add latency monitoring as a complementary FR to mitigate risk of adaptive bitrate impact on teleoperation responsiveness.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Adaptive bitrate algorithms can be implemented without unacceptable latency.
open_risks: Latency introduced by adaptive bitrate may affect teleoperation control.
Dependency on stable Network Link interface for communication quality.
evidence_needed: Performance data on packet loss and latency under adaptive bitrate control.
Verification of interface integration with Safety Services for safety alerts.
